page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.6xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 24 physical cores, resulting in 1.0 thread per core. It includes 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps and does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile lacks GPU acceleration, making it unsuitable for workloads requiring hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent CPU performance without resource sharing or burstable limits.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6.6xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise applications that require predictable CPU performance and a standard memory-to-core ratio. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU resources keeps the infrastructure profile focused on standard compute and memory tasks. This server is designed for workloads such as web serving, application servers, enterprise databases, and general-purpose business applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.5 GHz, it features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring consistent compute performance. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads, providing predictable performance without resource sharing. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and enterprise databases.
